figures.caption: Figure 1: LCFG Framework Overview. Left: Input context with heterogeneous LLM agents showing capability profiles across Math, Facts, and Logic dimensions. Center: Coalition-of-Thought (CoalT) reasoning module, a 5-step pipeline that guides agents through structured coalition evaluation. The latent space visualization shows agents transitioning from scattered initial positions to clustered Nash-stable coalitions through iterative refinement (h ← h + α · ∆h). The dashed border indicates the trainable/promptable module. Right: Output Nash-stable partition π∗ achieving 73.2% stability rate (+14.8pp over vanilla CoT). Key insight: Theorem 2 shows stability scales with preference consistency (pKeff ), not perfect rationality.
Table 3: Coalition formation results (400 episodes per condition). Statistical significance vs. Standard: ∗p < 0.01, ∗∗p < 0.001 (Wilcoxon, Bonferroni-corrected).

Figure 2: Nash stability rate vs. preference consistency across experimental conditions. Shaded region: range consistent with Theorem 2’s lower bound across plausible parameter settings. CoalT achieves highest consistency (p = 0.86) and stability (73.2%), confirming that stability scales with pKeff rather than perfect rationality.
